base_case: Davis v. Consolidated Rail Corp., 788 F.2d 1260 (7th Cir. 1986)
constructor: Venkat Porto (drafted by Claude Opus stand-in; auto-generated from base instance)

perturbations.original_text: The burden on Consolidated Rail of enforcing blue-flag protocols — training costs, supervisory oversight, the operational cost of ensuring every inspection is preceded by a properly-placed blue flag.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (burden of precaution) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f1 (burden_of_precaution). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The probability that a locomotive would couple to stationary cars while an inspector was beneath them, given the yard layout, the shift schedule, and the communication practices.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (probability of harm) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f2 (probability_of_harm). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The gravity of injury to a worker struck or crushed by a coupling locomotive — catastrophic injury, likely death.
